name: google-cloud
description: BigQuery analytics, Cloud Storage management, and data pipelines using Google Cloud
long_description:
Full Google Cloud Platform integration for Chitty Workspace. Query and manage BigQuery datasets, upload and organize files in Cloud Storage, and build data pipelines — all through natural language.

**BigQuery** — Run SQL queries, explore schemas, create datasets and tables, insert data.

**Cloud Storage** — Upload, download, list, and manage files in GCS buckets. Perfect for staging data for BigQuery or sharing results.

Authentication uses your existing `gcloud` CLI credentials. No passwords or service account keys are stored by Chitty.

**Security** — All mutating operations (write, delete, create) are disabled by default. Enable only the features you need via Feature Flags. Identifiers and paths are validated. Local file transfers are gated and sandboxed to workspace/temp directories.

auth.instructions: This package uses your Google Cloud CLI (gcloud) credentials. You'll be prompted to sign in via your browser during setup.

setup_steps.label: Check for Google Cloud CLI
setup_steps.help_text: Google Cloud CLI is required. It will be installed automatically.

setup_steps.label: Sign in to Google Cloud
setup_steps.help_text: Opens your browser to sign in with your Google account. No passwords are stored by Chitty.

setup_steps.label: Set default project
setup_steps.prompt_help: Enter your GCP project ID. Find it at console.cloud.google.com

setup_steps.label: Install Python dependencies
setup_steps.help_text: Chitty SDK provides authentication helpers and tool utilities for marketplace packages.

configurable_resources.label: BigQuery Datasets
configurable_resources.description: Select which BigQuery datasets the agent can access. The agent will only query, insert, or manage tables within these datasets.
configurable_resources.discover_command: python -c "import subprocess,json; token=subprocess.check_output(['gcloud','auth','print-access-token'],text=True).strip(); proj=subprocess.check_output(['gcloud','config','get-value','project'],text=True,stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL).strip(); import urllib.request; req=urllib.request.Request(f'https://bigquery.googleapis.com/bigquery/v2/projects/{proj}/datasets',headers={'Authorization':f'Bearer {token}'}); data=json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req).read()); print(json.dumps([{'id':d['datasetReference']['datasetId'],'name':d['datasetReference']['datasetId']} for d in data.get('datasets',[])]))"

configurable_resources.label: Cloud Storage Buckets
configurable_resources.description: Select which GCS buckets the agent can read from and write to.
configurable_resources.discover_command: python -c "import subprocess,json; token=subprocess.check_output(['gcloud','auth','print-access-token'],text=True).strip(); proj=subprocess.check_output(['gcloud','config','get-value','project'],text=True,stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL).strip(); import urllib.request; req=urllib.request.Request(f'https://storage.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b?project={proj}',headers={'Authorization':f'Bearer {token}'}); data=json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req).read()); print(json.dumps([{'id':d['name'],'name':d['name']} for d in data.get('items',[])]))"

feature_flags.label: Allow creating new datasets
feature_flags.description: When enabled, the agent can create new BigQuery datasets in your project.
feature_flags.enable_warning: Creating datasets may incur storage costs.

feature_flags.label: Allow deleting datasets
feature_flags.description: When enabled, the agent can delete BigQuery datasets and all their contents.
feature_flags.enable_warning: Deleting a dataset permanently removes all tables and data. This cannot be undone.

feature_flags.label: Allow mutating SQL queries
feature_flags.description: When enabled, the agent can run INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, CREATE, ALTER, and TRUNCATE SQL statements. When disabled, only SELECT queries are allowed.
feature_flags.enable_warning: Mutating queries can modify or delete data in your BigQuery datasets. Use with caution.

feature_flags.label: Allow creating new buckets
feature_flags.description: When enabled, the agent can create new Cloud Storage buckets.
feature_flags.enable_warning: Creating buckets may incur storage costs.

feature_flags.label: Allow deleting objects
feature_flags.description: When enabled, the agent can delete files from Cloud Storage buckets.
feature_flags.enable_warning: Deleted objects cannot be recovered unless versioning is enabled on the bucket.

feature_flags.label: Allow local file uploads to GCS
feature_flags.description: When enabled, the agent can read files from your local filesystem and upload them to Cloud Storage. Files are restricted to workspace and temp directories.
feature_flags.enable_warning: This allows the agent to read local files (within the workspace) and upload them to GCS.

feature_flags.label: Allow GCS downloads to local filesystem
feature_flags.description: When enabled, the agent can download files from Cloud Storage and save them to your local filesystem. Files are restricted to workspace and temp directories.
feature_flags.enable_warning: This allows the agent to write files to your local filesystem (within the workspace).

sub_agent_template.description_template: Focused BigQuery agent for the {display_name} dataset ({project_id}.{dataset_id})
sub_agent_template.persona_template:
You are a focused data analyst for the {display_name} BigQuery dataset ({project_id}.{dataset_id}).
Your tools are pre-scoped to this dataset.

Strategy:
1. Sample tables first to understand data shape (saves tokens and cost)
2. Use aggregations and metrics unless the user asks for specific records
3. Always add LIMIT clauses to avoid scanning excessive data
4. Format results as markdown tables for readability

When dispatched by parent agent: return concise data results.
When chatting directly with user: provide analysis and explanations.

agent_config.default_instructions: You have access to Google Cloud Platform tools. Always check the user's allowed datasets and buckets before performing operations. Prefer read-only operations (SELECT queries, list, describe) unless the user explicitly asks to modify data. Always confirm before running mutating queries, creating resources, or deleting anything. When querying BigQuery, add LIMIT clauses to avoid scanning excessive data.
suggested_prompts: List my BigQuery datasets and show me the tables
Run a SQL query on my dataset to find the top 10 rows
Upload this CSV file to my Cloud Storage bucket
Show me what's in my GCS bucket
capabilities: Run read-only SQL queries on BigQuery datasets
Explore table schemas and row counts
List bucket contents and object metadata
Upload inline text content to Cloud Storage
Upload/download local files (when enabled via feature flags)
Run mutating SQL queries (when enabled via feature flags)
Create and manage datasets and buckets (when enabled via feature flags)
